meta.title: AI Exposure of the Canadian Job Market
meta.primary_rollup_note: The dashboard rolls canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through the 45 official major groups.
meta.metric_model_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables are published at a coarser occupation grain than official NOC 2021 unit groups, so the unit-group labour metrics are allocated using ESDC unit employment weights. Outlook remains direct from the ESDC unit-group release.
meta.exposure_metric_scale: 0-10 display score derived from the share of workers in high-exposure EPIAC occupations
meta.exposure_note: Official EPIAC fields are mapped from published StatCan occupation groups onto the canonical NOC 2021 unit groups.
meta.profile_layer_note: Official OaSIS occupational profiles attach to canonical NOC 2021 unit groups through an explicit generated mapping table.
meta.source: Statistics Canada annual occupation tables rebuilt onto the canonical NOC 2021 spine

source_tables.name: 14-10-0416-01 Labour force characteristics by occupation, annual

oasis.title: Occupational and Skills Information System (OaSIS) - 2025 Version 1.0

family.title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
family.definition: This major group comprises administrative and financial supervisors; specialized administrative occupations; and accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations. These occupations come with Training, Education, Experience and Responsibility (TEER) identified as TEER 2. Occupations classified in TEER 2, as those in this group with a 2 as the second digit of their code, typically require completion of a post-secondary education program of two to three years at community college, institute of technology or CÉGEP; or completion of an apprenticeship training program of two to five years; or supervisory or significant safety responsibilities; or the accumulation of several years of experience and expertise in the subject matter knowledge in a related occupation found in TEER 3, when applicable (TEER 3 includes occupations with a 3 as a second digit in their code).
family.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
family.exposure_rationale: Aggregated from canonical unit groups. EPIAC values roll up unit-level mappings from published StatCan occupation groups.
family.metric_rationale: This major group aggregates canonical unit groups. Labour-market counts and wages reflect the unit-group estimates allocated from published StatCan annual occupation tables using ESDC employment weights.
family.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

units.title: Supervisors, general office and administrative support workers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Supervisors of general office and administrative support workers supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following minor groups: Office support and court services occupations (1410) and Survey, statistical and data entry occupations (1411). They are employed throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Supervisors, general office and administrative support workers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Supervisors, general office and administrative support workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors of general office and administrative support workers supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following minor groups: Office support and court services occupations (14.10) and Survey, statistical and data entry occupations (14.11).
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of college courses related to area supervised is usually required.
Experience in the occupation supervised is usually required.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to coordinate the work of clerks and activities with other units and departments
to identify errors or deviations in tasks such as data entry, record keeping and document preparation
to clearly communicate instructions, schedules and procedures to their team, to ensure all workers understand their tasks and can perform them correctly
to plan strategically by anticipating potential problems and developing proactive measures to address them
to provide guidance and direction to their team members and to inspire and motivate their team, fostering a positive work environment
to understand administrative concepts, principles, methods and procedures for managing day-to-day operations effectively

units.title: Supervisors, finance and insurance office workers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Supervisors of finance and insurance office workers supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Payroll administrators (13102), Accounting and related clerks (14200), Banking, insurance and other financial clerks (14201) and Collection clerks (14202). They are employed by banks and other financial institutions, insurance companies, and by other establishments throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Supervisors, finance and insurance office workers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Supervisors, finance and insurance office workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors of finance and insurance office workers supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Payroll administrators (13.102), Accounting and related clerks (14.200), Banking, insurance and other financial clerks (14.201) and Collection clerks (14.202).
employment_requirements: Completion of a college program or courses in bookkeeping, accounting or a related subject is required.
Experience in the clerical occupation supervised is required.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: assistant financial institution accountant - financial services
clerks supervisor - bank or financial institution

core_competencies.statement: to ensure efficient workflow and collaboration among team members, aligning tasks and priorities to achieve organizational goals
to review the work of their team, identify errors or inconsistencies and ensure tasks meet quality standards
to accurately handle and oversee financial transactions, such as accounts payable and receivable, payroll processing and insurance claims
to switch between different tasks, handle various types of financial documents, structure tasks logically and set priorities
to convey instructions, expectations and feedback to their team members and to communicate clearly with clients, vendors and other stakeholders
to ensure financial transactions, such as processing payments, recording transactions and managing accounts, are handled with accuracy and precision
to understand financial processes, including accounts payable and receivable, payroll management and financial reporting enables them to oversee these processes effectively

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Supervisors, library, correspondence and related information workers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors of library, correspondence and related information workers supervise and coordinate the activities of workers in the following unit groups: Survey interviewers and statistical clerks (14.110), Library assistants and clerks (14.300), and Correspondence, publication and regulatory clerks (14.301).
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
Completion of college courses related to area supervised may be required.
Experience in the clerical occupation supervised is usually required.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to ensure work processes are organized and efficient and to help avoid overlaps, delays and gaps in work
to assess the performance of team members and to help in providing constructive feedback, recognizing achievements and addressing areas where improvement is needed
to read and understand various documents, including reports, correspondence, policies and procedures
to ensure communication is clear and understood, reducing the likelihood of misunderstandings when relaying instructions to their team
to guide team members while ensuring that tasks are completed efficiently and to a high standard

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Library, Conservation, and Heritage
core_competencies.statement: to understand conservation techniques to effectively manage collections, including books, documents and other materials

They are employed throughout the public and private sectors.

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Supervisors, supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Supervisors, supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Supervisors of supply chain, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations supervise the activities of workers in unit group 13201 Production and transportation logistics coordinators and in sub-major group 144 Supply chain logistics, tracking and scheduling coordination occupations.
employment_requirements: Completion of a post-secondary education program of two to three years in business, supply chain, production or manufacturing management and related is usually required.
Several years of experience in the occupation supervised are usually required.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to ensure that all aspects of the supply chain and logistics processes are aligned
to meet production deadlines, ship schedules and customer delivery dates
to provide insights into the performance of team members and the overall functioning of operations
to allow for prompt solutions, minimizing disruptions and ensuring that operations run efficiently
to generate multiple potential solutions, evaluate their feasibility and choose the best course of action
to inspire their team, set goals and provide clear direction to ensure that all tasks are completed efficiently and on time
to understand how to manage the flow of goods, resources and information in order to ensure timely deliveries and cost-effective operations

units.title: Executive assistants
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Executive assistants coordinate administrative procedures, public relations activities and research and analysis functions for members of legislative assemblies, ministers, deputy ministers, corporate officials and executives, committees and boards of directors. They are employed by governments, corporations and associations.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Executive assistants coordinate administrative procedures, public relations activities and research and analysis functions for members of legislative assemblies, ministers, deputy ministers, corporate officials and executives, committees and boards of directors.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A bachelor's degree in public administration, political science or a related discipline is usually required.
Experience in a related administrative occupation is usually required.

core_competencies.statement: to manage and handle a wide range of materials, including documents, office supplies and equipment
to manage multiple tasks, schedules and projects, ensuring smooth operations and seamless collaboration among teams and stakeholders
to effectively communicate with executives, clients and teams, ensuring messages are understood and tasks are carried out efficiently
to ensure accurate transcription of verbal communications, which is crucial for maintaining precise records of meetings, discussions and official documents
to accurately manage schedules, correspondence and tasks, ensuring no important information or deadlines are overlooked
to ensure proper management of documents, including filing, indexing and retrieval

units.title: Human resources and recruitment officers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Human resources and recruitment officers identify and advertise job vacancies, recruit candidates, and assist in the selection and reassignment of employees. They are employed throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Human resources and recruitment officers identify and advertise job vacancies, recruit candidates, and assist in the selection and reassignment of employees.
employment_requirements: A college diploma in a field related to personnel management such as business administration, industrial relations, commerce or completion of a professional development program in personnel administration is usually required.
Certification as a Certified Human Resources Professional (CHRP) may be required.
Some experience in a clerical or administrative position related to personnel administration may be required.
additional_information: Progression to specialist and management positions is possible with experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to analyze issues, such as employee disputes or recruitment challenges and develop effective solutions
to gauge the needs, concerns and motivations of employees and candidates and to interpret non-verbal cues, emotions and underlying messages during interactions
to contact potential applicants to arrange interviews and notify applicants of the results of the selection process
to identify and resolve problems in the recruitment process, such as difficulties in attracting qualified candidates or issues with job descriptions
to draw on diverse expertise and viewpoints when addressing issues or challenges

core_competencies.competency: Knowledge in Human Resources and Labour relations
core_competencies.statement: to design and implement effective recruitment strategies, create accurate job descriptions and conduct fair and thorough selection processes

units.title: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers source and purchase general and specialized equipment, materials, business services and land or access rights for use or for further processing by their establishment. They are employed throughout the public and private sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. oasis_profiles.profile_title: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers source and purchase general and specialized equipment, materials, business services and land or access rights for use or for further processing by their establishment.
employment_requirements: Procurement and purchasing agents and officers usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in business administration, commerce or economics.

Procurement and purchasing agents and officers purchasing specialized materials or business services may require a related university degree or college diploma. For example, a bachelor's degree or college diploma in engineering may be required for purchasers of industrial products.
A certificate in purchasing from the Purchasing Management Association of Canada (PMAC) may be required.
Previous experience as a purchasing clerk or as an administrative clerk may be required.
Certification from the International Right of Way Association (IRWA) may be required.
Sourcing specialists and analysts usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in economics, logistics, operations management, finance, engineering, mathematics, or statistical analysis.
Previous work experience in supply chain for sourcing specialists and analysts is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions is possible with experience.
Procurement and purchasing agents and officers may specialize in the purchase of particular materials or business services such as furniture or access rights.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to secure better prices, discounts and favourable payment terms on items for the organization
to optimize inventory levels, reducing waste and avoiding overstock or stockouts
to negotiate prices, terms and conditions with suppliers and to collaborate with various departments to understand needs and convey procurement plans
to take proactive measures to prevent larger issues from developing, such as supply chain disruptions, quality issues or vendor reliability
to ensure all details are correct in purchase orders, such as item specifications, quantities and prices
to maintain records, track deliveries and manage correspondence with suppliers and teams, ensuring efficient operations and timely acquisition of goods and services

units.title: Conference and event planners
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Conference and event planners plan, organize and coordinate conferences, conventions, meetings, seminars, exhibitions, trade shows, festivals and other events. They are employed by tourism associations, trade and professional associations, convention and conference centres, governments and by conference and event planning companies, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Conference and event planners plan, organize and coordinate conferences, conventions, meetings, seminars, exhibitions, trade shows, festivals and other events.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business, tourism or hospitality administration is usually required.
Several years of experience in hospitality or tourism administration or in public relations are usually required and may substitute for formal education requirements.
Certification relating to special events, meetings or conference management may be required.

core_competencies.statement: to coordinate services such as accommodation, transportation, conference halls and other facilities, catering, audio-visual equipment and security
to secure better rates for venues, catering, entertainment and other services
to juggle various tasks such as budgeting, scheduling, liaising with vendors, securing venues and arranging accommodations
to communicate with clients, vendors and participants, ensuring all event details are clearly conveyed and understood
to ensure all aspects of an event are correctly covered, from scheduling to logistics
to understand client needs and preferences to ensure the event is tailored to meet their specific requirements and expectations

units.title: Employment insurance and revenue officers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.definition: Employment insurance and revenue officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to tax revenue, employment insurance and other government benefit services. They are employed by government agencies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Excise tax revenue officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to tax revenue.
main_duties: Audit accounting records to determine income, exemptions, payable taxes, compliance with reporting regulations and existence of fraud
Examine accounting systems and internal controls of organizations
Provide advice on reporting and evaluation methods for goods subject to taxation
Prepare briefs and assist in searching and seizing records, and in preparing charges for court cases.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree or college diploma is usually required.
Several years of related administrative or regulatory experience may be required.
Completion of specialized government training is required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions in these fields is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Government benefits services officers administer and enforce laws and regulations related to employment insurance and other government benefit services.
main_duties: Determine the eligibility of persons applying for government benefits such as Employment Insurance (EI), Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS)
Ascertain the facts on such issues as reasons for loss of employment and availability for work
Monitor the payments of benefits and investigate claimants when there appears to be fraud or abuse.
employment_requirements: A bachelor's degree or college diploma is usually required.
Several years of related administrative or regulatory experience may be required.
Completion of specialized government training is required.
additional_information: Progression to managerial positions in these fields is possible with experience.

units.title: Court reporters, medical transcriptionists and related occupations
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians and statistical officers
units.definition: Court reporters record and transcribe verbatim the proceedings of courts, legislative assemblies and committees, and prepare transcripts for use by judges, tribunals and quasi-judicial panels. They are employed by courts of law, provincial and federal legislative assemblies and committees, or they may be self-employed. Medical transcriptionists record, transcribe and edit dictation by physicians and other health care providers, surgical proceedings, health-related reports and other medical documentation. They are employed by hospitals, medical clinics and doctors' offices, or they may be self-employed. Closed captioners, braille technicians and other transcriptionists are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Court reporters, medical transcriptionists and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Court reporters record and transcribe verbatim the proceedings of courts, legislative assemblies and committees, and prepare transcripts for use by judges, tribunals and quasi-judicial panels.
main_duties: Record verbatim proceedings of courtroom using electronic stenograph machine or stenomask
Respond to requests during court sessions to read back verbatim evidence
Transcribe recorded proceedings in accordance with established formats
Verify accuracy of transcripts by checking copy against original record of proceedings and accuracy of rulings by checking with judge
Research and locate quotes to ensure accuracy
Respond to requests for transcripts ordered by judges, lawyers or the public
File and store shorthand notes of court session.
employment_requirements: Court reporters require completion of secondary school.
Completion of a college or other program in court reporting is usually requiredandthe Chartered Shorthand Reporter (CSR) certificate may be required for court reporters.
Occupations working in Braille usually require education in Unified English Braille (UEB) competence and related studies of 1-2 years.
additional_information: There is some mobility between occupations in this unit group and other occupations requiring similar knowledge of legal or medical terminology, such as legal or medical administrative positions.

oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Court reporters, medical transcriptionists and related occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Medical transcriptionists record, transcribe and edit dictation by physicians and other health care providers, surgical proceedings, health-related reports and other medical documentation.
main_duties: Transcribe recorded dictation in accordance with established formats or written documentation from medical reports, correspondence, health records and patient information
Proofread work for typographical and grammatical errors
Edit transcripts for missing or incorrect content and formatting.
employment_requirements: Medical transcriptionists require completion of secondary school.
Completion of a recognized medical transcription program is usually requiredandthe Certified Medical Transcriptionist (CMT) certificate may be required for medical transcriptors.
Occupations working in Braille usually require education in Unified English Braille (UEB) competence and related studies of 1-2 years.
additional_information: There is some mobility between occupations in this unit group and other occupations requiring similar knowledge of legal or medical terminology, such as legal or medical administrative positions.

units.title: Health information management occupations
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians and statistical officers
units.definition: Health information management workers collect, code, record, review and manage health information. They are employed by hospitals, clinics, workplace health and safety boards, health record consulting firms and other health care establishments. Health information management workers who are supervisors are included in this unit group.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Health information management workers collect, code, record, review and manage health information. Health information management occupations who are supervisors are included in this unit group.
employment_requirements: Health information management technicians and related workers require completion of a recognized two-year college diploma program in health record technology or health information management.

Health information management technicians and related workers are eligible to write the Canadian Health Information Management Association national certification examination which may be required by employers.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience.
Progression to management positions is possible with additional training and experience.

core_competencies.statement: to evaluate the accuracy and completeness of health records and to ensure data is reliable
to document patient information, medical histories, treatment plans and coding details
to input and code health information from medical records, forms and other documents that require reading and interpreting small text and numbers
to classify, code, cross-reference and store health records and other information
to discuss patient information, medical records and coding details with doctors, nurses and other healthcare professionals
to ensure all health information, including patient records and coding, is accurate
to organize and manage health records such as filing documents, updating records and ensuring information is easily accessible when needed

units.title: Records management technicians
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians and statistical officers
units.definition: Records management technicians operate and maintain systems for the collection, classification, retrieval and retention of records, images, documents and information. They are employed throughout the private and public sectors.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Records management technicians operate and maintain systems for the collection, classification, retrieval and retention of records, images, documents and information.
employment_requirements: Records management technicians usually require completion of a two- to three-year college program in information or records management technology.
Two to three years' experience in a records management environment may be required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with additional training and experience.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: technical indexer - records and file management

core_competencies.statement: to review and interpret records, archived documents, retrieval requests and security protocols, ensuring proper organization
to review files periodically to ensure they are complete and classified
to ensure records are correctly categorized and easily searchable, which is essential for maintaining accuracy in records management
to identify common attributes or trends in records for the purpose of creating standardized procedures for organizing and managing documents
to handle records with precision, ensuring information is correctly entered, categorized and retrieved
to understand how to handle, process and sort documents

units.title: Statistical officers and related research support occupations
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Court reporters, transcriptionists, records management technicians and statistical officers
units.definition: Statistical officers and workers in related research support occupations provide statistical and research support services to a wide range of businesses and organizations. These workers conduct statistical routines, monitor trends, compile data and prepare charts, graphs, summaries and reports in support of organizational information needs and research activities. They are employed throughout the public and private sectors. Statistical officers who are also supervisors are included in this unit group.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.profile_title: Statistical officers and related research support occupations
oasis_profiles.unit_group_title: Statistical officers and related research support occupations
oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Statistical officers and workers in related research support occupations provide statistical and research support services to a wide range of businesses and organizations. These workers conduct statistical routines, monitor trends, compile data and prepare charts, graphs, summaries and reports in support of organizational information needs and research activities.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A college diploma or undergraduate degree in a social science, commerce, statistics or a related field is usually required.
additional_information: Progression to supervisory positions is possible with experience. Progression to professional occupations in statistics and research usually requires completion of a postgraduate degree.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

core_competencies.statement: to manage, organize and store large datasets using digital tools and software that includes databases, spreadsheets and data management systems
to read and understand various data sources, including research papers, reports and datasets
to apply techniques such as regression analysis, probability theory and hypothesis testing to derive meaningful insights from raw data
to organize and analyze various types of data from different sources effectively, regardless of its format or structure
to ensure calculations are correct and that the results of statistical analyses are reliable
to understand and apply various statistical methods, such as hypothesis testing, regression analysis and correlation

units.title: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.definition: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers maintain complete sets of books, keep records of accounts, verify the procedures used for recording financial transactions, and provide personal bookkeeping services. They are employed throughout the private and public sectors, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Accounting technicians and bookkeepers maintain complete sets of books, keep records of accounts, verify the procedures used for recording financial transactions, and provide personal bookkeeping services.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.

Completion of a college program in accounting, bookkeeping or a related field orcompletion of two years (first level) of a recognized professional accounting program (e.g., Chartered Accounting, Certified General Accounting) orcourses in accounting or bookkeeping combined with several years of experience as a financial or accounting clerk are required.
additional_information: Progression to professional accounting occupations is possible with additional training.
workplaces_employers: Establishements throughout the private and public sector

example_titles.job_title_text: financial services administrator – Canadian armed forces

core_competencies.statement: to calculate and prepare cheques for payrolls and for utility, tax and other bills, as well as to prepare financial statements
to analyze financial data, identify patterns and risks and apply evidence-based solutions that strengthen risk awareness and sound financial practices
to identify and correct errors in financial records by logically analyzing transactions and balances
to post journal entries, reconcile accounts, prepare trial balance of books and maintain general ledgers
to ensure all financial calculations, such as totals, percentages and ratios, are performed accurately
to ensure all financial transactions are recorded correctly and avoid significant discrepancies, which can affect financial statements and decision-making
to accurately record financial transactions, ensuring all entries are properly classified and recorded in the correct accounts

units.title: Insurance adjusters and claims examiners
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.definition: Insurance adjusters investigate insurance claims and determine the amount of loss or damages covered by insurance policies. They are employed in claims departments of insurance companies or as independent adjusters. Insurance claims examiners examine claims investigated by insurance adjusters and authorize payments. They are employed at head offices or branches of insurance companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Insurance adjusters investigate insurance claims and determine the amount of loss or damages covered by insurance policies.
main_duties: Investigate circumstances surrounding insurance claims to determine validity of claim
Inspect automobile, home or other property damage
Take statements and consult with claimants, accident witnesses, doctors and other relevant individuals and examine records or reports
Determine amount of loss or damages covered by insurance policies
Negotiate settlement of claims
Prepare adjustment reports.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A bachelor's degree, college diploma or some post-secondary education orseveral years of experience as a clerk in the claims department or other general insurance experience is required.
Several years of on-the-job training and completion of insurance industry courses and training programs are required.
Independent adjusters require a provincial licence issued by the Superintendent of Insurance in the province or territory of employment.
additional_information: Completion of educational programs through the Insurance Institute of Canada or its provincial counterparts entitles insurance adjusters and claims examiners to professional recognition as a Chartered Insurance Professional (CIP), and with additional university courses, as a Fellow Chartered Insurance Professional (FCIP).

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Insurance claims examiners examine claims investigated by insurance adjusters and authorize payments.
main_duties: Review, examine, calculate and authorize insurance claims investigated by insurance adjusters
Examine adjusters' reports and similar insurance claims or precedents to determine extent of insurance coverage
Ensure claims are valid and settlements are made according to company practices and procedures
Consult lawyers, doctors or other relevant individuals to discuss insurance claims
Approve automobile, fire, life, disability, dental or other insurance claims.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is required.
A bachelor's degree, college diploma or some post-secondary education orseveral years of experience as a clerk in the claims department or other general insurance experience is required.
Several years of on-the-job training and completion of insurance industry courses and training programs are required.

units.title: Insurance underwriters
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.definition: Insurance underwriters review and evaluate insurance applications to determine insurance risks, insurance premiums and extent of insurance coverage according to company policies. They are employed at head and branch offices of insurance companies.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.

units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Insurance underwriters review and evaluate insurance applications to determine insurance risks, insurance premiums and extent of insurance coverage according to company policies.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school and some general insurance experience ora bachelor's degree, college diploma or some post-secondary education is required.
Several years of on-the-job training and completion of insurance industry underwriting courses and training programs are required.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in the insurance industry is possible with experience.

Completion of educational programs through the Insurance Institute of Canada or its provincial counterparts entitles insurance underwriters to professional recognition as a Chartered Insurance Professional (CIP) and, with additional university courses, as a Fellow, Chartered Insurance Professional (FCIP).

core_competencies.statement: to ensure the processes are reliable and the underwriting processes adhere to company policies and regulatory requirements
to evaluate these scenarios from various angles, weigh evidence and make well-informed decisions about coverage and premiums
to evaluate new and renewal insurance applications to determine risks, premiums, coverage and other conditions of insurance contracts
to evaluate numerical data such as statistics, probability and historical trends to assess the likelihood of potential claims
to meticulously review applications and related documents to accurately assess the risk associated with insuring a particular individual or entity
to understand financial principles insuring an individual or entity and setting premiums that reflect that risk while ensuring profitability for the insurer

units.title: Assessors, business valuators and appraisers
units.major_group_title: Administrative and financial supervisors and specialized administrative occupations
units.sub_major_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.minor_group_title: Accounting, insurance and related business administrative occupations
units.definition: Assessors and appraisers determine the value of land, businesses, estates and other real property, for purposes of sale, purchase, taxation or disposal of assets. Appraisers also determine the value of personal and household items. Business valuators quantify the value of a business, its securities or its intangible assets. Assessors, business valuators and appraisers are employed by government agencies, real estate firms and other private companies, or they may be self-employed.
units.metric_source_note: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.metric_rationale: StatCan annual labour-force and wage tables do not publish this official NOC 2021 unit group directly. Labour-market counts are estimated by allocating the published StatCan annual occupation tables across official unit groups using ESDC outlook employment weights. Rates and wage fields inherit published source-group values until a direct canonical StatCan release is added.
units.exposure_rationale: Mapped to the closest published StatCan EPIAC occupation group from the 2021 Census (NOC 2016): Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13).
units.epiac_source_title: Experimental Estimates of Potential Artificial Intelligence Occupational Exposure in Canada, 2024
epiac_source_groups: Administrative occupations in finance, insurance and business (12, 13)

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Assessors determine the value of land, businesses, estates and other real property, for purposes of sale, purchase, taxation and disposal of assets.
main_duties: Assess value of land, building, structures, machinery, equipment and property improvements for purposes of taxation, grants and regional planning and prior to sale or purchase
Review and analyze data such as past sales, title searches, engineering and alignment maps, soil maps, subdivision plans, water and sewer plans, leasing cost data and easements
Explain assessment process to ratepayers.
employment_requirements: Assessors require a college diploma in municipal assessment andaccreditation with the municipal assessment association of the province.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in each field is possible with experience.
Assessors may specialize in advising and consulting in a specific field of assessment such as residential, commercial, industrial, institutional or farm lands.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Business valuators quantify the value of a business, its securities or its intangible assets.
main_duties: Collect and analyze financial records of a business, such as financial statements, budgets, projections and sales data, to assess its competitiveness or to estimate its market value
Prepare reports for estate planning, matrimonial litigation, insurance and business losses
Appear as an expert witness before courts, security regulators and other regulatory boards and commissions.
employment_requirements: Business valuators usually require a bachelor's degree or college diploma in accounting, business or commerce andaccreditation with the Canadian Institute of Chartered Business Valuators based on successful completion of its business and security valuation program.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in each field is possible with experience.

oasis_profiles.lead_statement: Appraisers determine the value of property, building, industrial and commercial machinery and equipment and personal and household items.
main_duties: Appraise value of property, building, industrial and commercial machinery and equipment and personal and household items
Prepare appraisal reports for lending agencies, insurance companies, government departments, courts, attorneys, creditors, buyers or auctioneers.
employment_requirements: Appraisers require a college diploma or vocational training in the field of appraisal orseveral years of experience in a position related to the field of appraisal.
In Quebec, membership with l'Ordre des évaluateurs agréés du Québec (OEAQ) is required to use the title chartered appraiser.
Real estate appraisers usually require several years of experience as a real estate agent, right-of-way agent or property administrator and may require Canadian residential appraiser certification.
Appraisers of residential, commercial or industrial property require accreditation with the Appraisal Institute of Canada.
additional_information: Progression to management positions in each field is possible with experience.
Appraisers may specialize in a specific type of appraisal such as real estate appraisal.
